title: Covering indexes do not work in update joins
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)
```
CREATE TABLE a (
  id int(11) not NULL,
  bal decimal(10,4) DEFAULT NULL,
  bal2 float DEFAULT NULL,
  primary KEY  (id) NONCLUSTERED
) ;

CREATE TABLE b (
  id int(11) NOT NULL,
  bal decimal(18,4)  ,
  bal2 decimal(18,1) DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (id) NONCLUSTERED 
  ,key idx2(id, bal)
);

explain
update /*+ inl_join(b) use_index(b,idx2) */ a 
join b on a.id = b.id 
set a.bal = b.bal; 
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)
when table a join table b,  just access the index idx2 of table b，without the need for a table lookup. 

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
```
id                              |estRows |task     |access object               |operator info                                                                                                  |
--------------------------------+--------+---------+----------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
Update_6                        |N/A     |root     |                            |N/A                                                                                                            |
└─Projection_7                  |12500.00|root     |                            |test.a.id, test.a.bal, test.a.bal2, test.a._tidb_rowid, test.b.id, test.b.bal, test.b.bal2, test.b._tidb_rowid |
  └─IndexJoin_12                |12500.00|root     |                            |inner join, inner:IndexLookUp_11, outer key:test.a.id, inner key:test.b.id, equal cond:eq(test.a.id, test.b.id)|
    ├─TableReader_32(Build)     |10000.00|root     |                            |data:TableFullScan_31                                                                                          |
    │ └─TableFullScan_31        |10000.00|cop[tikv]|table:a                     |keep order:false, stats:pseudo                                                                                 |
    └─IndexLookUp_11(Probe)     |12500.00|root     |                            |                                                                                                               |
      ├─IndexRangeScan_9(Build) |12500.00|cop[tikv]|table:b, index:idx2(id, bal)|range: decided by [eq(test.b.id, test.a.id)], keep order:false, stats:pseudo                                   |
      └─TableRowIDScan_10(Probe)|12500.00|cop[tikv]|table:b                     |keep order:false, stats:pseudo                                                                                 |
```
### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
Release Version: v7.5.3
Edition: Community
Git Commit Hash: 70bfd90035cc81e80d78b4f1f76ca4baef8a5756
Git Branch: HEAD
UTC Build Time: 2024-07-31 13:17:12
GoVersion: go1.21.10
Race Enabled: false
Check Table Before Drop: false
Store: tikv
key_bug_features: Covering indexes fail in update joins
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE c (id int(11) NOT NULL, val varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (id) NONCLUSTERED);
CREATE TABLE d (id int(11) NOT NULL, val decimal(10,2) DEFAULT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (id) NONCLUSTERED, KEY idx3(id, val));
INSERT INTO c (id, val) VALUES (1, '100.00'), (2, '200.50'), (3, NULL);
INSERT INTO d (id, val) VALUES (1, 100.00), (2, 200.50), (3, NULL);
UPDATE /*+ inl_join(d) use_index(d,idx3) */ c JOIN d ON c.id = d.id SET c.val = CAST(d.val AS CHAR);
